from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Mapping of predicates to human‑readable phrase generators
    phrase_map = {
        "cityServed": lambda o: f"serves the city of {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": lambda o: f"is at an elevation of {o} above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": lambda o: f"is at an elevation of {o} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": lambda o: f"is at an elevation of {o} metres above sea level",
        "location": lambda o: f"is located in {o}",
        "operatingOrganisation": lambda o: f"is operated by {o}",
        "runwayLength": lambda o: f"has a runway length of {o} metres",
        "runwayName": lambda o: f"has a runway named {o}",
        "country": lambda o: f"is in {'the ' + o if o.startswith('United') or o == 'Netherlands' or o == 'Philippines' else o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda o: f"is part of {o}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": lambda o: f"has a first runway length of {o} feet",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"has a first runway surface of {o}",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": lambda o: f"has a third runway length of {o} feet",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": lambda o: f"has ICAO code {o}",
        "locationIdentifier": lambda o: f"has IATA code {o}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": lambda o: f"has IATA code {o}",
        "nativeName": lambda o: f"is also known as {o}",
        "leaderParty": lambda o: f"is led by the {o}",
        "capital": lambda o: f"has {o} as its capital",
        "language": lambda o: f"speaks {o.replace(' language','')}",
        "leader": lambda o: f"is led by {o}",
        "owner": lambda o: f"is owned by {o}",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": lambda o: f"has a first runway length of {o} metres",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"has a fourth runway surface of {o}",
        "5thRunwayNumber": lambda o: f"has a fifth runway number {o}",
        "largestCity": lambda o: f"has {o} as its largest city",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": lambda o: f"has a fourth runway length of {o} feet",
        "1stRunwayNumber": lambda o: f"has a first runway number {o}",
        "administrativeArrondissement": lambda o: f"is in the administrative arrondissement {o}",
        "mayor": lambda o: f"has mayor {o}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"has a second runway surface of {o}",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"has a third runway surface of {o}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"has runway surface {o}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda o: f"official language is {o}",
        "city": lambda o: f"is located in {o}",
        "jurisdiction": lambda o: f"has jurisdiction over {o}",
        "demonym": lambda o: f"people are called {o}",
        "aircraftHelicopter": lambda o: f"operates helicopter {o}",
        "transportAircraft": lambda o: f"operates transport aircraft {o}",
        "currency": lambda o: f"uses currency {o}",
        "headquarter": lambda o: f"has headquarters at {o}",
        "class": lambda o: f"belongs to class {o}",
        "division": lambda o: f"belongs to division {o}",
        "order": lambda o: f"belongs to order {o}",
        "regionServed": lambda o: f"serves the region of {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda o: f"has the leader title of {o}",
        "hubAirport": lambda o: f"has hub airport at {o}",
        "aircraftFighter": lambda o: f"operates fighter {o}",
        "attackAircraft": lambda o: f"operates attack aircraft {o}",
        "battle": lambda o: f"was involved in the battle of {o}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"has a fifth runway surface of {o}",
        "countySeat": lambda o: f"has county seat {o}",
        "chief": lambda o: f"has chief {o}",
        "foundedBy": lambda o: f"was founded by {o}",
        "postalCode": lambda o: f"has postal code {o}",
        "areaCode": lambda o: f"has area code {o}",
        "foundingYear": lambda o: f"was founded in {o}",
        "ceremonialCounty": lambda o: f"is in ceremonial county {o}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject to avoid repetition
    subject_triples: dict[str, list[Triple]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subject_triples:
            subject_triples[t.subject] = []
        subject_triples[t.subject].append(t)

    # Process each triple and build phrases
    parts: list[str] = []
    for t in triples:
        s, p, o = t.subject, t.predicate, t.object

        generator = phrase_map.get(p)
        if generator:
            phrase = generator(o)
        elif p.endswith("RunwayLengthFeet"):
            rn = p.split("Runway")[0].lower()
            phrase = f"has a {rn} runway length of {o} feet"
        elif p.endswith("RunwayLengthMetre"):
            rn = p.split("Runway")[0].lower()
            phrase = f"has a {rn} runway length of {o} metres"
        elif p.endswith("RunwaySurfaceType"):
            rn = p.split("Runway")[0].lower()
            phrase = f"has a {rn} runway surface of {o}"
        elif p.endswith("RunwayNumber"):
            rn = p.split("Runway")[0].lower()
            phrase = f"has a {rn} runway number {o}"
        else:
            phrase = f"{p} {o}"

        parts.append(f"{s} {phrase}")

    # Join all parts into a single fluent complex sentence with relative clauses
    if len(parts) == 0:
        return ""
    elif len(parts) == 1:
        sentence = parts[0] + "."
    elif len(parts) == 2:
        # Check if both parts are about the same subject
        if parts[0].startswith(parts[1].split()[0]):
            sentence = parts[0] + ", which " + parts[1].split(" ", 1)[1] + "."
        else:
            sentence = parts[0] + ", and " + parts[1] + "."
    else:
        # Create complex sentence with relative clauses for better flow
        sentence = parts[0]
        prev_subject = parts[0].split()[0]
        for i, part in enumerate(parts[1:], 1):
            current_subject = part.split()[0]
            if current_subject == prev_subject:
                # Same subject - use relative clause
                sentence += ", which " + part.split(" ", 1)[1]
            elif i == len(parts) - 1:
                # Last part - use ", and" for final conjunction
                sentence += ", and " + part
            else:
                # Middle parts - use "; " for clause separation
                sentence += "; " + part
            prev_subject = current_subject
        sentence += "."

    # Ensure the first character is capitalised
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
